---
title: "LRPGF05: Factors associated with gains in phonetic spelling (PS) — off-floor"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRPGF05 is the **gain-factors** model for phonetic spelling (`spphon`).

_Floored outcome - off-floor likelihood._ Phonetic spelling is **heavily floored** (most period post-scores are zero), so a
graded Beta-Binomial gain would be driven by a few dispersed tail values rather
than the factor contrasts. Following the suite's floor rule, the likelihood is a
**Bernoulli on the binary off-floor indicator** (period post $> 0$): the linear
predictor models the **log-odds of being off the floor at post** — pooling
zero→positive movement, staying off the floor, and return-to-floor together —
**not** the log-odds of "coming off the floor". `beta_trt` is the off-floor
log-odds of being on intervention, and its items-scale marginal collapses to the
**off-floor risk difference** (reported with a provisional δ pending education-lead
sign-off).

_Causal labelling (DAG guardrails)._ Only the randomised on-intervention term is causal. Under the revised DAG
(`dag/dag-language-reading.dagitty`, revised 2026-07-10) the **period-1 average
marginal effect** — the genuinely randomised, all-untreated-baseline transition —
is the ITT-anchor estimand; `beta_trt` itself is only the on-intervention log-odds
contrast, not the ITT effect. Own baseline, age, ability (blocks), the upstream
skills letter sounds (L) and blending (B), and the phonological-memory confounder
(RW = `erbto`, with a missing-indicator) are **adjusted associations**: the child
random intercept is a partial, shrunken stand-in for between-child heterogeneity
and does **not** control latent general ability, so these internal slopes stay
descriptive - "associated with", never "drives". Sessions/dose are never
conditioned on (collider); SES is excluded (non-DAG / redundant).

## Model

$$
(P_{\text{post}} > 0) \sim \operatorname{Bernoulli}(\operatorname{expit}(\eta)),\quad
\eta = \alpha + \alpha_{\text{period}} + \beta_{\text{trt}}\text{OnIntervention}
  + \gamma_{\text{own}}\operatorname{logit}_*(P_{\text{pre}}) + \gamma_A A_{\text{std}}
  + \gamma_{\text{ability}}z(\text{blocks}) + \sum_{s\in\{L,B\}}\gamma_s\operatorname{logit}_*(s_{\text{pre}})
  + \sum_{c}\gamma_c\,z(c)
  + \textstyle\sum\text{interactions} + u_{\text{child}}.
$$

Here $\sum_{c}\gamma_c\,z(c)$ is the standardised phonological-memory confounder
(RW = `erbto`), entered with a missing-indicator. No Beta-Binomial concentration
(`kappa`) under the off-floor Bernoulli. Priors follow the shared template in
`priors.py`.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_results_factors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
